42	class BaseGrammarObject:
43	
44	    def __init__(self):
45	        self._finished = False
46	        self.grammar_stats = None
47	        self.current_token = None
48	
49	    def maybe_init_reasoning(self, reasoning: bool):
50	        pass
51	
52	    def accept_token(self, token: int) -> None:
53	        """
54	        Accept a token in the grammar.
55	        """
56	        raise NotImplementedError()
57	
58	    def rollback(self, k: int):
59	        raise NotImplementedError()
60	
61	    def is_terminated(self):
62	        return False
63	
64	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
65	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
66	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
67	        raise NotImplementedError()
68	
69	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
70	        raise NotImplementedError()
71	
72	    @staticmethod
73	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
74	        raise NotImplementedError()
75	
76	    @staticmethod
77	    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None:
78	        raise NotImplementedError()
79	
80	    def copy(self) -> "BaseGrammarObject":
81	        return self
82	
83	    @property
84	    def finished(self):
85	        return self._finished
86	
87	    @finished.setter
88	    def finished(self, finished):
89	        self._finished = finished
90	
91	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple[List[int], str]]:
92	        """
93	        Try to jump forward in the grammar.
94	
95	        Returns:
96	            A jump forward helper which may be used in `jump_forward_str_state`.
97	            None if the jump forward is not possible.
98	        """
99	        raise NotImplementedError()
100	
101	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
102	        """
103	        Jump forward for the grammar.
104	
105	        Returns:
106	            A tuple of the jump forward string and the next state of the grammar
107	            (which can be used in `jump_and_retokenize` if needed).
108	        """
109	        raise NotImplementedError()
110	
111	    def jump_and_retokenize(
112	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
113	    ) -> None:
114	        """
115	        Jump forward occurs, and update the grammar state if needed.
116	        """
117	        raise NotImplementedError()

120	INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ = BaseGrammarObject()

123	@dataclass
124	class CacheEntry:
125	    value: BaseGrammarObject
126	    event: Event
127	
128	
129	class BaseGrammarBackend:
130	    def __init__(self):
131	        self.executor = ThreadPoolExecutor()
132	        self.cache: Dict[Tuple[str, str], CacheEntry] = {}
133	
134	    def _not_supported(self, key_type: str, key_string: str) -> None:
135	        logger.warning(f"Skip unsupported {key_type=}, {key_string=}")
136	
137	    def dispatch_fallback(
138	        self, key_type: str, key_string: str
139	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
140	        """
141	        This function should not be reached in any case.
142	        """
143	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid key_type: {key_type}={key_string}")
144	
145	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
146	        return self._not_supported("json", key_string)
147	
148	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
149	        return self._not_supported("regex", key_string)
150	
151	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
152	        return self._not_supported("ebnf", key_string)
153	
154	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
155	        return self._not_supported("structural_tag", key_string)
156	
157	    def _init_value_dispatch(
158	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
159	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
160	        s = time.perf_counter()
161	        key_type, key_string = key
162	        if key_type == "json":
163	            grammar = self.dispatch_json(key_string)
164	        elif key_type == "regex":
165	            grammar = self.dispatch_regex(key_string)
166	        elif key_type == "ebnf":
167	            grammar = self.dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
168	        elif key_type == "structural_tag":
169	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
170	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern":
171	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern(key_string)
172	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern_v2":
173	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern_v2(key_string)
174	        else:
175	            grammar = self.dispatch_fallback(key_type, key_string)
176	
177	        if grammar is not None and grammar.grammar_stats is not None:
178	            grammar.grammar_stats.compilation_time = time.perf_counter() - s
179	        return grammar
180	
181	    def get_cached_or_future_value(
182	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
183	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
184	        value = self.cache.get(key)
185	        if value:
186	            copied_value = value.copy()
187	            copied_value.maybe_init_reasoning(require_reasoning)
188	            return copied_value, True
189	        value = self.executor.submit(self._init_value_dispatch, key, require_reasoning)
190	        return value, False
191	
192	    def set_cache(self, key: Tuple[str, str], value: BaseGrammarObject):
193	        self.cache[key] = value
194	
195	    def reset(self):
196	        self.cache.clear()
197

199	GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY = {}
200	
201	
202	def register_grammar_backend(name, init_func):
203	    GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name] = init_func
204	
205	
206	def create_grammar_backend(
207	    server_args: ServerArgs,
208	    tokenizer,
209	    vocab_size: int,
210	    eos_token_ids: Optional[set] = None,
211	) -> Optional[BaseGrammarBackend]:
212	    name = server_args.grammar_backend
213	
214	    # Custom grammar backend has the highest priority
215	    if name in GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY:
216	        return GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name](
217	            server_args, tokenizer, vocab_size, eos_token_ids
218	        )
219	
220	    # Default grammar backends
221	    if name == "outlines":
222	        from sglang.srt.constrained.outlines_backend import OutlinesGrammarBackend
223	
224	        grammar_backend = OutlinesGrammarBackend(
225	            tokenizer,
226	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
227	        )
228	    elif name == "xgrammar":
229	        from sglang.srt.constrained.xgrammar_backend import (
230	            TokenizerNotSupportedError,
231	            XGrammarGrammarBackend,
232	        )
233	
234	        # Convert Set[int] to List[int] if needed
235	        eos_list = list(eos_token_ids) if eos_token_ids else None
236	
237	        try:
238	            grammar_backend = XGrammarGrammarBackend(
239	                tokenizer,
240	                vocab_size=vocab_size,
241	                model_eos_token_ids=eos_list,
242	                any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
243	            )
244	        except TokenizerNotSupportedError as e:
245	            logger.warning(
246	                f"Grammar backend disabled because tokenizer is not supported by XGrammar: {e}. "
247	                "Falling back to grammar_backend='none'. "
248	                "Structured outputs (JSON schema, regex, EBNF) will not be available."
249	            )
250	            server_args.grammar_backend = "none"
251	            return None
252	    elif name == "llguidance":
253	        from sglang.srt.constrained.llguidance_backend import GuidanceBackend
254	
255	        grammar_backend = GuidanceBackend(
256	            tokenizer=tokenizer,
257	            any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
258	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
259	        )
260	    elif name == "none":
261	        return None
262	    else:
263	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid grammar backend: {name}")
264	
265	    if server_args.reasoning_parser and hasattr(tokenizer, "think_end_id"):
266	        from sglang.srt.constrained.reasoner_grammar_backend import (
267	            ReasonerGrammarBackend,
268	        )
269	
270	        grammar_backend = ReasonerGrammarBackend(
271	            grammar_backend, tokenizer.think_end_id
272	        )
273	
274	    return grammar_backend

776	        """
777	        Construct sampling param based on default + override values
778	
779	        The default values of sampling are populated in `default_sampling_para` via sgl.function.run(...sampling_args)

782	        Here we use default_sampling_para as the base and override the values if they exist in `sampling_params`.

I cannot write to that path due to sandbox restrictions. I will present the full implementation plan directly in my response instead.

---

## Implementation Plan: Adding `json_path` Output Constraint to SGLang

### Design Decisions

**1. Mutual exclusivity**: Yes, `json_path` must be mutually exclusive with `json_schema`, `regex`, and `ebnf`. Since `json_path` is internally converted to `json_schema`, having both set simultaneously would be contradictory. This follows the same pattern as the existing mutual exclusivity check at `sampling_params.py` line 155-161.

**2. JSONPath subset to support**: A practical subset covering the most common query patterns:
- `$.key` -- simple property access
- `$.key1.key2` -- nested property access  
- `$.key[*].field` -- array with items having required field
- `$.key[0].field` -- array index (treated the same as `[*]` for schema purposes, since we are constraining structure not values)
- `$[*]` -- root is an array
- Wildcard `*` at leaf means "any type" (no constraint on the leaf value type)

**3. Conversion location**: The conversion from `json_path` to `json_schema` should happen in `grammar_manager.py` inside `process_req_with_grammar()`, BEFORE the cache key is created. This way:
- All entry points benefit (OpenAI API, native `/generate`, SGLang frontend, gRPC)
- The grammar backend never sees `json_path` -- it only sees `json_schema`
- The cache key is `("json", <generated_schema>)`, so identical schemas from different json_paths are deduplicated

**4. Error messages**: Yes, `json_path` should be mentioned in the "grammar backend disabled" error message alongside the others.

---

### Step-by-Step Implementation

#### Step 1: Create `python/sglang/srt/constrained/json_path.py`

This is a new file containing the JSONPath-to-JSON-Schema converter. It is a pure function with no external dependencies beyond the standard library.

The module should contain:

**Function `json_path_to_json_schema(json_path: str) -> str`**

Algorithm:
1. Validate the input starts with `$`
2. Tokenize the path into segments: property names (from `.name`), array markers (from `[*]` or `[N]`)
3. Build a nested JSON Schema from outside in
4. Return the schema as a JSON string

Parsing logic:
- Split after the leading `$` into segments
- `.propertyName` becomes `{"type": "object", "required": ["propertyName"], "properties": {"propertyName": <inner>}}`
- `[*]` or `[N]` becomes `{"type": "array", "items": <inner>}`
- The innermost (leaf) schema is `{}` (any type allowed)

Edge cases:
- `$` alone should return `{}` (any structure)
- Raise `ValueError` for invalid paths (missing `$`, empty segments, unmatched brackets)
- Property names containing special characters should be quoted properly (they become JSON keys, which already support any string)

A simple recursive or iterative builder works well. Since the path is short, no performance concerns. Example implementation sketch:

```python
import json
import re

def json_path_to_json_schema(json_path: str) -> str:
    if not json_path.startswith("$"):
        raise ValueError(f"JSONPath must start with '$', got: {json_path}")
    
    rest = json_path[1:]  # strip leading $
    segments = _tokenize(rest)
    schema = _build_schema(segments)
    return json.dumps(schema)

def _tokenize(path: str) -> list:
    # Returns list of ("property", name) or ("array",) tuples
    segments = []
    i = 0
    while i < len(path):
        if path[i] == '.':
            # Property access
            i += 1
            match = re.match(r'[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*', path[i:])
            if not match:
                raise ValueError(f"Invalid property name at position {i}")
            segments.append(("property", match.group()))
            i += match.end()
        elif path[i] == '[':
            # Array access
            end = path.index(']', i)
            segments.append(("array",))
            i = end + 1
        else:
            raise ValueError(f"Unexpected character '{path[i]}' at position {i}")
    return segments

def _build_schema(segments: list) -> dict:
    if not segments:
        return {}  # leaf: any type
    
    seg = segments[0]
    inner = _build_schema(segments[1:])
    
    if seg[0] == "property":
        return {
            "type": "object",
            "required": [seg[1]],
            "properties": {seg[1]: inner}
        }
    elif seg[0] == "array":
        return {
            "type": "array",
            "items": inner
        }
```

This file should also include **unit-testable validation**. Add a `validate_json_path(json_path: str) -> None` function that raises `ValueError` for invalid input, called at the start of `json_path_to_json_schema`.

#### Step 2: Modify `python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py`

Two changes:

Then mix this into the test classes in `test/registered/constrained_decoding/test_constrained_decoding.py`.

---

### Implementation Order and Dependencies

1. **Step 1** (json_path.py) -- no dependencies, can be done first
2. **Step 2** (sampling_params.py) -- no dependencies on Step 1
3. **Step 3** (grammar_manager.py) -- depends on Steps 1 and 2
4. **Steps 4-6** (ir.py, api.py, interpreter.py) -- depend on Step 2, can be done in parallel
5. **Step 8** (protocol.py, serving_completions.py) -- depend on Step 2
6. **Step 10** (tests) -- depends on all above

Steps 1, 2, 4, 5, 6, and 8 can mostly be done in parallel since they are independent additions of the `json_path` field at different layers. Step 3 ties them together.

---

### Potential Challenges

1. **JSONPath specification ambiguity**: JSONPath has no single standard (RFC 9535 was only published in 2024). The implementation should be explicit about which subset is supported and document it clearly. Sticking to the basic subset described above avoids most ambiguity.

2. **Cache key deduplication**: Since `json_path` is converted to `json_schema` before the cache key is created, two different json_path expressions that produce the same schema will share cache entries. This is desirable behavior.

3. **Positional argument ordering in `SglGen`**: The `SglGen.__init__` and the `gen()` function use positional arguments extensively. Adding `json_path` after `json_schema` must be done carefully to ensure all call sites pass arguments correctly. The `gen_int()` and `gen_string()` helper functions pass `None` for `regex` and `json_schema` positionally -- they would need `None` added for `json_path` as well.

4. **SglSamplingParams.clone()**: Uses positional arguments. Adding `json_path` means updating the clone method's positional argument list. This is fragile; a future refactor to use `dataclasses.replace()` or `**asdict()` would be better, but the current change should match the existing pattern.

---

- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775391892354-m3r5wc/python/sglang/lang/ir.py` -- the IR-level sampling params and SglGen that thread json_path through the frontend

I need to design an implementation plan for adding a `json_path` output constraint to SGLang. Based on thorough codebase exploration, here's the full context:

## Architecture Context

SGLang has a multi-layer constraint pipeline:

**User API layer** (`python/sglang/lang/api.py`):
- `gen()` function accepts constraint params: `regex`, `json_schema`, `dtype`, `choices`
- Creates `SglGen` objects with `SglSamplingParams`

**IR layer** (`python/sglang/lang/ir.py`):
- `SglSamplingParams` dataclass holds: `json_schema`, `dtype`, `regex`
- `SglGen` class stores params in `self.sampling_params`
- `to_srt_kwargs()` passes `regex` and `json_schema` to backend

**Frontend layer** (`python/sglang/lang/backend/runtime_endpoint.py`):
- `_handle_dtype_to_regex()` converts dtype→regex BEFORE sending to server. This is a precedent for "convert one constraint type to another".

**Interpreter layer** (`python/sglang/lang/interpreter.py`):
- `_resolve_sampling_params()` at line 775 merges defaults with overrides for a list of fields including `dtype`, `regex`, `json_schema`

**Server SamplingParams** (`python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py`):
- `SamplingParams.__init__()` accepts: `json_schema`, `regex`, `ebnf`, `structural_tag`
- `verify()` checks mutual exclusivity: `grammars = [self.json_schema, self.regex, self.ebnf]`

**Grammar Manager** (`python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py`):
- `process_req_with_grammar()` checks `json_schema`, `regex`, `ebnf`, `structural_tag`
- Creates cache keys like `("json", json_schema_str)`, `("regex", regex_str)`, etc.
- Passes to grammar backend

**Grammar Backend** (`python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py`):
- `_init_value_dispatch()` dispatches to `dispatch_json()`, `dispatch_regex()`, `dispatch_ebnf()`, etc.
- Concrete backends (xgrammar, outlines, llguidance) implement these dispatch methods
- All backends already support `dispatch_json()` for JSON schema constraints

**OpenAI Protocol** (`python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py`):
- `CompletionRequest` has fields: `json_schema`, `regex`, `ebnf`
- `to_sampling_params()` extracts these into a dict

## Design Decision

The key insight is that **json_path can be converted to json_schema**. A JSONPath pattern like `$.store.book[*].author` maps to a JSON Schema:
```json
{
  "type": "object",
  "required": ["store"],
  "properties": {
    "store": {
      "type": "object", 
      "required": ["book"],
      "properties": {
        "book": {
          "type": "array",
          "items": {
            "type": "object",
            "required": ["author"],
            "properties": {"author": {}}
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

This is analogous to how `dtype` is converted to `regex` in the frontend. The conversion can happen in `grammar_manager.py` on the server side, so all entry points (OpenAI API, SGLang frontend, direct API) benefit.

## Files to Modify

1. **NEW: `python/sglang/srt/constrained/json_path.py`** - JSONPath-to-JSON-Schema converter
2. **`python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py`** - Add `json_path` parameter
3. **`python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py`** - Route json_path → json_schema
4. **`python/sglang/lang/ir.py`** - Add to SglSamplingParams and SglGen  
5. **`python/sglang/lang/api.py`** - Add to gen() function
6. **`python/sglang/lang/interpreter.py`** - Add to _resolve_sampling_params field list
7. **`python/sglang/lang/backend/runtime_endpoint.py`** - Pass json_path through to_srt_kwargs
8. **`python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py`** - Add json_path to CompletionRequest/ChatCompletionRequest

## Questions to Address in Plan

1. Should json_path be mutually exclusive with json_schema/regex/ebnf? (I think yes — it converts to json_schema internally, so they'd conflict)
2. What JSONPath subset should we support? (Basic: `$.key`, `$.key1.key2`, `$.key[*].field`, `$.key[0].field`)
3. Where exactly should the conversion happen? (I propose in grammar_manager.py, converting json_path to json_schema before creating the cache key)
4. Should we add json_path to the error messages about grammar backend being disabled?

Please design a detailed, step-by-step implementation plan with exact code changes for each file.

I cannot write to that path due to sandbox restrictions. I will present the full implementation plan directly in my response instead.

---

## Implementation Plan: Adding `json_path` Output Constraint to SGLang

### Design Decisions

**1. Mutual exclusivity**: Yes, `json_path` must be mutually exclusive with `json_schema`, `regex`, and `ebnf`. Since `json_path` is internally converted to `json_schema`, having both set simultaneously would be contradictory. This follows the same pattern as the existing mutual exclusivity check at `sampling_params.py` line 155-161.

**2. JSONPath subset to support**: A practical subset covering the most common query patterns:
- `$.key` -- simple property access
- `$.key1.key2` -- nested property access  
- `$.key[*].field` -- array with items having required field
- `$.key[0].field` -- array index (treated the same as `[*]` for schema purposes, since we are constraining structure not values)
- `$[*]` -- root is an array
- Wildcard `*` at leaf means "any type" (no constraint on the leaf value type)

**3. Conversion location**: The conversion from `json_path` to `json_schema` should happen in `grammar_manager.py` inside `process_req_with_grammar()`, BEFORE the cache key is created. This way:
- All entry points benefit (OpenAI API, native `/generate`, SGLang frontend, gRPC)
- The grammar backend never sees `json_path` -- it only sees `json_schema`
- The cache key is `("json", <generated_schema>)`, so identical schemas from different json_paths are deduplicated

**4. Error messages**: Yes, `json_path` should be mentioned in the "grammar backend disabled" error message alongside the others.

---

### Step-by-Step Implementation

#### Step 1: Create `python/sglang/srt/constrained/json_path.py`

This is a new file containing the JSONPath-to-JSON-Schema converter. It is a pure function with no external dependencies beyond the standard library.

The module should contain:

**Function `json_path_to_json_schema(json_path: str) -> str`**

Algorithm:
1. Validate the input starts with `$`
2. Tokenize the path into segments: property names (from `.name`), array markers (from `[*]` or `[N]`)
3. Build a nested JSON Schema from outside in
4. Return the schema as a JSON string

Parsing logic:
- Split after the leading `$` into segments
- `.propertyName` becomes `{"type": "object", "required": ["propertyName"], "properties": {"propertyName": <inner>}}`
- `[*]` or `[N]` becomes `{"type": "array", "items": <inner>}`
- The innermost (leaf) schema is `{}` (any type allowed)

Edge cases:
- `$` alone should return `{}` (any structure)
- Raise `ValueError` for invalid paths (missing `$`, empty segments, unmatched brackets)
- Property names containing special characters should be quoted properly (they become JSON keys, which already support any string)

A simple recursive or iterative builder works well. Since the path is short, no performance concerns. Example implementation sketch:

```python
import json
import re

def json_path_to_json_schema(json_path: str) -> str:
    if not json_path.startswith("$"):
        raise ValueError(f"JSONPath must start with '$', got: {json_path}")
    
    rest = json_path[1:]  # strip leading $
    segments = _tokenize(rest)
    schema = _build_schema(segments)
    return json.dumps(schema)

def _tokenize(path: str) -> list:
    # Returns list of ("property", name) or ("array",) tuples
    segments = []
    i = 0
    while i < len(path):
        if path[i] == '.':
            # Property access
            i += 1
            match = re.match(r'[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*', path[i:])
            if not match:
                raise ValueError(f"Invalid property name at position {i}")
            segments.append(("property", match.group()))
            i += match.end()
        elif path[i] == '[':
            # Array access
            end = path.index(']', i)
            segments.append(("array",))
            i = end + 1
        else:
            raise ValueError(f"Unexpected character '{path[i]}' at position {i}")
    return segments

def _build_schema(segments: list) -> dict:
    if not segments:
        return {}  # leaf: any type
    
    seg = segments[0]
    inner = _build_schema(segments[1:])
    
    if seg[0] == "property":
        return {
            "type": "object",
            "required": [seg[1]],
            "properties": {seg[1]: inner}
        }
    elif seg[0] == "array":
        return {
            "type": "array",
            "items": inner
        }
```

This file should also include **unit-testable validation**. Add a `validate_json_path(json_path: str) -> None` function that raises `ValueError` for invalid input, called at the start of `json_path_to_json_schema`.

    # Convert json_path to json_schema
    if req.sampling_params.json_path is not None:
        try:
            req.sampling_params.json_schema = json_path_to_json_schema(
                req.sampling_params.json_path
            )
            req.sampling_params.json_path = None
        except ValueError as e:
            req.set_finish_with_abort(f"Invalid json_path: {e}")
            return False
    
    if (
        req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
        or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
        or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
        or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
    ):

Add `json_path: Optional[str] = None` parameter after `json_schema` (line 474), and pass it through to `SglSamplingParams`:

```

Note: The `clone()` method on `SglSamplingParams` (line 42) does NOT include `dtype` or `regex` in its field list -- those are described as "not included in to_xxx_kwargs." The `json_path` field is similar to `json_schema` which IS included in clone. So `json_path` should be added to clone as well. However, looking more carefully, `clone()` uses positional args which is fragile. The `json_path` should be added after `json_schema` in the clone call.

#### Step 5: Modify `python/sglang/lang/api.py`

**5b. Pass `json_path` to `SglGen` constructor** (line 117):

#### Step 10: Add Tests

**10a. Unit tests for `json_path.py` converter:**

Create `test/srt/constrained/test_json_path.py`:

Test cases:
- `$.store.book[*].author` produces the nested schema from the design doc
- `$.name` produces `{"type": "object", "required": ["name"], "properties": {"name": {}}}`
- `$[*]` produces `{"type": "array", "items": {}}`
- `$` produces `{}`
- Invalid paths raise `ValueError`: missing `$`, empty property name, unmatched brackets
- Mutual exclusivity: setting both `json_path` and `json_schema` raises error in `SamplingParams.verify()`

**10b. Integration test:**

Add a test case to the existing `TestJSONConstrainedMixin` or create a new `TestJSONPathConstrainedMixin` in `python/sglang/test/kits/`:

```python
class TestJSONPathConstrainedMixin:
    def test_json_path_generate(self):
        response = requests.post(
            self.base_url + "/generate",
            json={
                "text": "Generate a person with name and age as JSON.",
                "sampling_params": {
                    "temperature": 0,
                    "max_new_tokens": 128,
                    "json_path": "$.name",
                },
            },
        )
        ret = response.json()
        obj = json.loads(ret["text"])
        self.assertIn("name", obj)
```

Then mix this into the test classes in `test/registered/constrained_decoding/test_constrained_decoding.py`.

---

### Implementation Order and Dependencies

1. **Step 1** (json_path.py) -- no dependencies, can be done first
2. **Step 2** (sampling_params.py) -- no dependencies on Step 1
3. **Step 3** (grammar_manager.py) -- depends on Steps 1 and 2
4. **Steps 4-6** (ir.py, api.py, interpreter.py) -- depend on Step 2, can be done in parallel
5. **Step 8** (protocol.py, serving_completions.py) -- depend on Step 2
6. **Step 10** (tests) -- depends on all above

Steps 1, 2, 4, 5, 6, and 8 can mostly be done in parallel since they are independent additions of the `json_path` field at different layers. Step 3 ties them together.

---

### Potential Challenges

1. **JSONPath specification ambiguity**: JSONPath has no single standard (RFC 9535 was only published in 2024). The implementation should be explicit about which subset is supported and document it clearly. Sticking to the basic subset described above avoids most ambiguity.

2. **Cache key deduplication**: Since `json_path` is converted to `json_schema` before the cache key is created, two different json_path expressions that produce the same schema will share cache entries. This is desirable behavior.

3. **Positional argument ordering in `SglGen`**: The `SglGen.__init__` and the `gen()` function use positional arguments extensively. Adding `json_path` after `json_schema` must be done carefully to ensure all call sites pass arguments correctly. The `gen_int()` and `gen_string()` helper functions pass `None` for `regex` and `json_schema` positionally -- they would need `None` added for `json_path` as well.

4. **SglSamplingParams.clone()**: Uses positional arguments. Adding `json_path` means updating the clone method's positional argument list. This is fragile; a future refactor to use `dataclasses.replace()` or `**asdict()` would be better, but the current change should match the existing pattern.

---

### Critical Files for Implementation

- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775391892354-m3r5wc/python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py` -- the conversion dispatch point where json_path becomes json_schema
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775391892354-m3r5wc/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py` -- the server-side parameter class that must accept and validate json_path
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775391892354-m3r5wc/python/sglang/lang/ir.py` -- the IR-level sampling params and SglGen that thread json_path through the frontend
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775391892354-m3r5wc/python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py` -- the OpenAI-compatible request models that expose json_path to API users
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775391892354-m3r5wc/python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/serving_completions.py` -- the completion endpoint that must pass json_path into the sampling params dict

class ChatCompletionRequest(BaseModel):
    # Ordered by official OpenAI API documentation
    # https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create
    messages: List[ChatCompletionMessageParam]
    model: str = Field(
        default=DEFAULT_MODEL_NAME,
        description="Model name. Supports LoRA adapters via 'base-model:adapter-name' syntax.",
    )
    frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
    logit_bias: Optional[Dict[str, float]] = None
    logprobs: bool = False
    top_logprobs: Optional[int] = None
    max_tokens: Optional[int] = Field(
        default=None,
        deprecated="max_tokens is deprecated in favor of the max_completion_tokens field",
        description="The maximum number of tokens that can be generated in the chat completion. ",
    )
    max_completion_tokens: Optional[int] = Field(
        default=None,
        description="The maximum number of completion tokens for a chat completion request, "
        "including visible output tokens and reasoning tokens. Input tokens are not included. ",
    )
    n: int = 1
    presence_penalty: float = 0.0
    response_format: Optional[Union[ResponseFormat, StructuralTagResponseFormat]] = None
    seed: Optional[int] = None
    stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None
    stream: bool = False
    stream_options: Optional[StreamOptions] = None
    temperature: Optional[float] = None
    top_p: Optional[float] = None
    user: Optional[str] = None
    tools: Optional[List[Tool]] = Field(default=None, examples=[None])
    tool_choice: Union[ToolChoice, Literal["auto", "required", "none"]] = Field(
        default="auto", examples=["none"]
    )  # noqa
    return_hidden_states: bool = False
    return_routed_experts: bool = False
    return_cached_tokens_details: bool = False
    reasoning_effort: Optional[Literal["low", "medium", "high"]] = Field(
        default="medium",
        description="Constrains effort on reasoning for reasoning models. "
        "'low' is the least effort, 'high' is the most effort. Reducing reasoning effort can "
        "result in faster responses and fewer tokens used on reasoning in a response. "
        "Currently only supported for OpenAI models in the harmony path, i.e GPT-OSS models.",
    )

    # Extra parameters for SRT backend only and will be ignored by OpenAI models.
    top_k: Optional[int] = None
    min_p: Optional[float] = None
    min_tokens: int = 0
    regex: Optional[str] = None
    ebnf: Optional[str] = None
    repetition_penalty: Optional[float] = None
    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None
    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None
    no_stop_trim: bool = False
    ignore_eos: bool = False
    continue_final_message: bool = False
    skip_special_tokens: bool = True
    lora_path: Optional[Union[List[Optional[str]], Optional[str]]] = None
    session_params: Optional[Dict] = None
    separate_reasoning: bool = True
    stream_reasoning: bool = True
    chat_template_kwargs: Optional[Dict] = None

    # SGLang multimodal tiling controls (extensions)
    max_dynamic_patch: Optional[int] = None
    min_dynamic_patch: Optional[int] = None

    # Custom logit processor for advanced sampling control
    custom_logit_processor: Optional[Union[List[Optional[str]], str]] = None
    custom_params: Optional[Dict] = None

    # For request id
    rid: Optional[Union[List[str], str]] = None
    # Extra key for classifying the request (e.g. cache_salt)
    extra_key: Optional[Union[List[str], str]] = None
    # Cache salt for request caching
    cache_salt: Optional[Union[List[str], str]] = None
    # Priority for the request
    priority: Optional[int] = None

    # For PD disaggregation
    bootstrap_host: Optional[Union[List[str], str]] = None
    bootstrap_port: Optional[Union[List[Optional[int]], int]] = None
    bootstrap_room: Optional[Union[List[int], int]] = None

    # For data parallel rank routing
    data_parallel_rank: Optional[int] = None

    # OpenAI/SGLang default sampling parameters
    _DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS = {
        "temperature": 1.0,
        "top_p": 1.0,
        "top_k": -1,
        "min_p": 0.0,
        "repetition_penalty": 1.0,
    }

    @model_validator(mode="before")
    @classmethod
    def set_tool_choice_default(cls, values):
        if values.get("tool_choice") is None:

            else:

        if r is None:
            return values

        if isinstance(r, dict):
            effort = r.get("effort") or r.get("reasoning_effort")
            if effort in {"low", "medium", "high"}:

            "logit_bias": request.logit_bias,
            "custom_params": request.custom_params,
            "sampling_seed": request.seed,

}

        return values

    def to_sampling_params(
        self,
        stop: List[str],
        model_generation_config: Dict[str, Any],
        tool_call_constraint: Optional[ToolCallConstraint] = None,
    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """
        Convert request to sampling parameters.
        Priority: user value > model generation_config > OpenAI defaults
        """

        def get_param(param_name: str):
            value = getattr(self, param_name)
            if value is None:
                return model_generation_config.get(
                    param_name, self._DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS[param_name]
                )
            return value

        # add per user request
        spaces_between_special_tokens = (
            True
            if self.chat_template_kwargs is None
            else self.chat_template_kwargs.get("spaces_between_special_tokens", True)
        )

        sampling_params = {
            "temperature": get_param("temperature"),
            "max_new_tokens": self.max_completion_tokens or self.max_tokens,
            "min_new_tokens": self.min_tokens,
            "stop": stop,
            "stop_token_ids": self.stop_token_ids,
            "stop_regex": self.stop_regex,
            "top_p": get_param("top_p"),
            "top_k": get_param("top_k"),
            "min_p": get_param("min_p"),
            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,
            "frequency_penalty": self.frequency_penalty,
            "repetition_penalty": get_param("repetition_penalty"),
            "regex": self.regex,
            "ebnf": self.ebnf,
            "n": self.n,
            "no_stop_trim": self.no_stop_trim,
            "ignore_eos": self.ignore_eos,
            "skip_special_tokens": self.skip_special_tokens,
            "logit_bias": self.logit_bias,
            "custom_params": self.custom_params,
            "sampling_seed": self.seed,
            "spaces_between_special_tokens": spaces_between_special_tokens,
        }

        if self.response_format and self.response_format.type == "json_schema":
            sampling_params["json_schema"] = convert_json_schema_to_str(
                self.response_format.json_schema.schema_
            )
        elif self.response_format and self.response_format.type == "json_object":
            sampling_params["json_schema"] = '{"type": "object"}'
        elif self.response_format and self.response_format.type == "structural_tag":
            sampling_params["structural_tag"] = convert_json_schema_to_str(
                self.response_format.model_dump(by_alias=True)
            )

        # Check if there are already existing output constraints
        has_existing_constraints = (
            sampling_params.get("regex")
            or sampling_params.get("ebnf")
            or sampling_params.get("structural_tag")
            or sampling_params.get("json_schema")
        )

        if tool_call_constraint and has_existing_constraints:
            logger.warning("Constrained decoding is not compatible with tool calls.")
        elif tool_call_constraint:
            constraint_type, constraint_value = tool_call_constraint
            if constraint_type == "structural_tag":
                sampling_params[constraint_type] = convert_json_schema_to_str(
                    constraint_value.model_dump(by_alias=True)
                )
            elif constraint_type == "json_schema":
                sampling_params[constraint_type] = convert_json_schema_to_str(
                    constraint_value  # type: ignore
                )
            else:
                sampling_params[constraint_type] = constraint_value

        return sampling_params

# Plan: Add `json_path` Output Constraint to SGLang

## Context

SGLang supports constrained decoding via grammar-based constraints (`regex`, `json_schema`, `ebnf`). These constraints restrict LLM output token-by-token using finite state machines. We need to add a new `json_path` constraint that accepts a JSONPath pattern (e.g., `"$.store.book[*].author"`) and ensures the generated output is valid JSON containing the specified structural path.

**Key design decision:** Convert `json_path` to `json_schema` at the server level in `grammar_manager.py`, before dispatch. This mirrors how `dtype` is converted to `regex` in the frontend. All existing grammar backends (xgrammar, outlines, llguidance) already support JSON schema, so no backend changes are needed.

---

## Files to Modify

| # | File | Action |
|---|------|--------|
| 1 | `python/sglang/srt/constrained/json_path.py` | **CREATE** — JSONPath-to-JSON-Schema converter |
| 2 | `python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py` | Add `json_path` param + mutual exclusivity |
| 3 | `python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py` | Convert `json_path` → `json_schema` before dispatch |
| 4 | `python/sglang/lang/ir.py` | Add `json_path` to `SglSamplingParams` + `SglGen` |
| 5 | `python/sglang/lang/api.py` | Add `json_path` to `gen()` function |
| 6 | `python/sglang/lang/interpreter.py` | Add `"json_path"` to `_resolve_sampling_params` field list |
| 7 | `python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py` | Add `json_path` to `CompletionRequest` + `ChatCompletionRequest` |
| 8 | `python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/serving_completions.py` | Add `json_path` to `_build_sampling_params` |
| 9 | `python/sglang/test/srt/constrained/test_json_path.py` | **CREATE** — Unit tests for converter |

---

## Step 1: Create `python/sglang/srt/constrained/json_path.py`

New module with a `json_path_to_json_schema(json_path: str) -> str` function.

**Algorithm:**
1. Validate input starts with `$`
2. Tokenize the rest into segments: `.propertyName` → `("property", name)`, `[*]` or `[N]` → `("array",)`
3. Build nested JSON schema from outside-in
4. Return as JSON string

**Conversion rules:**
- `.name` → `{"type": "object", "required": ["name"], "properties": {"name": <inner>}}`
- `[*]` or `[0]` → `{"type": "array", "items": <inner>}`
- Leaf (end of path) → `{}` (any type)

**Examples:**
- `"$.store.book[*].author"` → `{"type":"object","required":["store"],"properties":{"store":{"type":"object","required":["book"],"properties":{"book":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"object","required":["author"],"properties":{"author":{}}}}}}}}`
- `"$.name"` → `{"type":"object","required":["name"],"properties":{"name":{}}}`
- `"$"` → `{}` (any valid JSON)

**Supported JSONPath subset:**
- `$` — root
- `.key` — property access (alphanumeric + underscore)
- `[*]` — array wildcard
- `[N]` — array index (treated same as `[*]` for schema purposes)

**Error handling:** Raise `ValueError` for invalid paths (no `$` prefix, empty property names, unmatched brackets).

```python
"""Utility to convert JSONPath expressions to JSON Schema for constrained decoding."""

import json
import re
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Tuple


def json_path_to_json_schema(json_path: str) -> str:
    """Convert a JSONPath expression to a JSON Schema string.

    The generated schema ensures the LLM output is valid JSON containing
    the structural path described by the JSONPath expression.

    Supported JSONPath subset:
        - ``$``           root (any valid JSON)
        - ``.key``        property access
        - ``[*]``         array wildcard
        - ``[N]``         array index (treated as wildcard for schema)

    Args:
        json_path: A JSONPath expression starting with ``$``.

    Returns:
        A JSON string representing the equivalent JSON Schema.

    Raises:
        ValueError: If the JSONPath expression is invalid.
    """
    if not isinstance(json_path, str) or not json_path.startswith("$"):
        raise ValueError(
            f"JSONPath must be a string starting with '$', got: {json_path!r}"
        )

    rest = json_path[1:]
    segments = _tokenize(rest)
    schema = _build_schema(segments)
    return json.dumps(schema)


def _tokenize(path: str) -> List[Tuple[str, ...]]:
    segments: List[Tuple[str, ...]] = []
    i = 0
    while i < len(path):
        if path[i] == ".":
            i += 1
            match = re.match(r"[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*", path[i:])
            if not match:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"Invalid property name at position {i} in path '${path}'"
                )
            segments.append(("property", match.group()))
            i += match.end()
        elif path[i] == "[":
            close = path.find("]", i)
            if close == -1:
                raise ValueError(f"Unmatched '[' at position {i} in path '${path}'")
            segments.append(("array",))
            i = close + 1
        else:
            raise ValueError(
                f"Unexpected character '{path[i]}' at position {i} in path '${path}'"
            )
    return segments


def _build_schema(segments: List[Tuple[str, ...]]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    if not segments:
        return {}
    seg = segments[0]
    inner = _build_schema(segments[1:])
    if seg[0] == "property":
        return {
            "type": "object",
            "required": [seg[1]],
            "properties": {seg[1]: inner},
        }
    elif seg[0] == "array":
        return {
            "type": "array",
            "items": inner,
        }
    else:
        raise ValueError(f"Unknown segment type: {seg[0]}")
```

---

## Step 2: Modify `python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py`

**2a.** Add `json_path: Optional[str] = None` parameter to `__init__` (after `json_schema` on line 55):
```python
json_path: Optional[str] = None,
```
And store it: `self.json_path = json_path` (after line 85).

**2b.** Update mutual exclusivity check in `verify()` (lines 155–161):
```python
grammars = [
    self.json_schema,
    self.json_path,
    self.regex,
    self.ebnf,
]
if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
    raise ValueError(
        "Only one of regex, json_schema, json_path, or ebnf can be set."
    )
```

---

## Step 3: Modify `python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py`

**3a.** Add import at top:
```python
from sglang.srt.constrained.json_path import json_path_to_json_schema
```

**3b.** Insert json_path → json_schema conversion at the start of `process_req_with_grammar()`, between `add_to_grammar_queue = False` (line 69) and the existing `if` block (line 70):
```python
# Convert json_path to json_schema before grammar processing
if req.sampling_params.json_path is not None:
    try:
        req.sampling_params.json_schema = json_path_to_json_schema(
            req.sampling_params.json_path
        )
        req.sampling_params.json_path = None
    except ValueError as e:
        req.set_finish_with_abort(f"Invalid json_path: {e}")
        return False
```

This converts before the existing condition check, so the existing `json_schema` processing path handles everything downstream. No other changes to this method needed.

**3c.** Update error message on line 77 to mention `json_path`:
```
"Grammar-based generation (json_schema, json_path, regex, ebnf, structural_tag) is not supported when the server is launched with --grammar-backend none"
```

---

## Step 4: Modify `python/sglang/lang/ir.py`

**4a.** Add field to `SglSamplingParams` (after `json_schema` at line 36):
```python
json_path: Optional[str] = None
```

**4b.** Add `json_path` to `clone()` method (line 42). Currently `clone()` passes positional args ending with `self.json_schema` at line 61. Add `self.json_path` after it. But note: `clone()` does NOT include `dtype` or `regex` — those are described as "for constrained generation, not included in to_xxx_kwargs". Since `json_path` IS a grammar constraint like `json_schema`, include it:
```python
self.json_path,
```

**4c.** Add to `to_srt_kwargs()` return dict (after `"json_schema"` at line 137):
```python
"json_path": self.json_path,
```

**4d.** Add `json_path` parameter to `SglGen.__init__()` (after `json_schema` at line 474):
```python
json_path: Optional[str] = None,
```
And pass it to `SglSamplingParams(...)`:
```python
json_path=json_path,
```

---

## Step 5: Modify `python/sglang/lang/api.py`

**5a.** Add `json_path` parameter to `gen()` (after `json_schema` at line 98):
```python
json_path: Optional[str] = None,
```

**5b.** Pass it to `SglGen(...)` constructor (after `json_schema` at line 138):
```python
json_path,
```

---

## Step 6: Modify `python/sglang/lang/interpreter.py`

Add `"json_path"` to the field list in `_resolve_sampling_params()` (after `"json_schema"` at line 809):
```python
"json_path",
```

---

## Step 7: Modify `python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py`

**7a.** Add to `CompletionRequest` (after `json_schema` at line 268):
```python
json_path: Optional[str] = None
```

**7b.** Add to `ChatCompletionRequest` (after `ebnf` at line 581):
```python
json_path: Optional[str] = None
```

**7c.** Add `json_path` to `has_existing_constraints` check in `ChatCompletionRequest.to_sampling_params()` (line 762):
```python
has_existing_constraints = (
    sampling_params.get("regex")
    or sampling_params.get("ebnf")
    or sampling_params.get("structural_tag")
    or sampling_params.get("json_schema")
    or sampling_params.get("json_path")
)
```

**7d.** Add to `ChatCompletionRequest.to_sampling_params()` dict (after `"ebnf"` at line 739):
```python
"json_path": self.json_path,
```

---

## Step 8: Modify `python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/serving_completions.py`

Add to `_build_sampling_params()` dict (after `"json_schema"` at line 144):
```python
"json_path": request.json_path,
```

---

## Step 9: Create `python/sglang/test/srt/constrained/test_json_path.py`

Unit tests for the converter:

```python
import json
import unittest
from sglang.srt.constrained.json_path import json_path_to_json_schema

class TestJsonPathToJsonSchema(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_root_only(self):
        self.assertEqual(json.loads(json_path_to_json_schema("$")), {})

    def test_simple_property(self):
        schema = json.loads(json_path_to_json_schema("$.name"))
        self.assertEqual(schema["type"], "object")
        self.assertIn("name", schema["required"])

    def test_nested_properties(self):
        schema = json.loads(json_path_to_json_schema("$.store.book"))
        self.assertEqual(schema["properties"]["store"]["properties"]["book"], {})

    def test_array_with_item_property(self):
        schema = json.loads(json_path_to_json_schema("$.store.book[*].author"))
        book = schema["properties"]["store"]["properties"]["book"]
        self.assertEqual(book["type"], "array")
        self.assertIn("author", book["items"]["required"])

    def test_root_array(self):
        schema = json.loads(json_path_to_json_schema("$[*]"))
        self.assertEqual(schema["type"], "array")

    def test_invalid_no_dollar(self):
        with self.assertRaises(ValueError):
            json_path_to_json_schema("store.book")

    def test_invalid_empty_property(self):
        with self.assertRaises(ValueError):
            json_path_to_json_schema("$.")

    def test_invalid_unmatched_bracket(self):
        with self.assertRaises(ValueError):
            json_path_to_json_schema("$.store[")
```

---

## Verification

1. **Unit tests:** `python -m pytest python/sglang/test/srt/constrained/test_json_path.py -v`
2. **Import check:** `python -c "from sglang.srt.constrained.json_path import json_path_to_json_schema; print(json_path_to_json_schema('$.store.book[*].author'))"`
3. **Mutual exclusivity:** `python -c "from sglang.srt.sampling.sampling_params import SamplingParams; p = SamplingParams(json_path='$.x', regex='.*'); p.verify(32000)"` — should raise `ValueError`
4. **API surface:** `python -c "from sglang.lang.api import gen; g = gen('x', json_path='$.name'); print(g.sampling_params.json_path)"` — should print `$.name`

# Plan: Add `json_path` Output Constraint to SGLang

## Context

SGLang supports constrained decoding via grammar-based constraints (`regex`, `json_schema`, `ebnf`). These constraints restrict LLM output token-by-token using finite state machines. We need to add a new `json_path` constraint that accepts a JSONPath pattern (e.g., `"$.store.book[*].author"`) and ensures the generated output is valid JSON containing the specified structural path.

**Key design decision:** Convert `json_path` to `json_schema` at the server level in `grammar_manager.py`, before dispatch. This mirrors how `dtype` is converted to `regex` in the frontend. All existing grammar backends (xgrammar, outlines, llguidance) already support JSON schema, so no backend changes are needed.

---

## Files to Modify

|---|------|--------|
| 1 | `python/sglang/srt/constrained/json_path.py` | **CREATE** — JSONPath-to-JSON-Schema converter |
| 2 | `python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py` | Add `json_path` param + mutual exclusivity |
| 3 | `python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py` | Convert `json_path` → `json_schema` before dispatch |
| 4 | `python/sglang/lang/ir.py` | Add `json_path` to `SglSamplingParams` + `SglGen` |
| 5 | `python/sglang/lang/api.py` | Add `json_path` to `gen()` function |
| 6 | `python/sglang/lang/interpreter.py` | Add `"json_path"` to `_resolve_sampling_params` field list |
| 7 | `python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py` | Add `json_path` to `CompletionRequest` + `ChatCompletionRequest` |
| 8 | `python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/serving_completions.py` | Add `json_path` to `_build_sampling_params` |
| 9 | `python/sglang/test/srt/constrained/test_json_path.py` | **CREATE** — Unit tests for converter |

---

## Step 1: Create `python/sglang/srt/constrained/json_path.py`

New module with a `json_path_to_json_schema(json_path: str) -> str` function.

**Algorithm:**
1. Validate input starts with `$`
2. Tokenize the rest into segments: `.propertyName` → `("property", name)`, `[*]` or `[N]` → `("array",)`
3. Build nested JSON schema from outside-in
4. Return as JSON string

**Conversion rules:**
- `.name` → `{"type": "object", "required": ["name"], "properties": {"name": <inner>}}`
- `[*]` or `[0]` → `{"type": "array", "items": <inner>}`
- Leaf (end of path) → `{}` (any type)

**Examples:**
- `"$.store.book[*].author"` → `{"type":"object","required":["store"],"properties":{"store":{"type":"object","required":["book"],"properties":{"book":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"object","required":["author"],"properties":{"author":{}}}}}}}}`
- `"$.name"` → `{"type":"object","required":["name"],"properties":{"name":{}}}`
- `"$"` → `{}` (any valid JSON)

**Supported JSONPath subset:**
- `$` — root
- `.key` — property access (alphanumeric + underscore)
- `[*]` — array wildcard
- `[N]` — array index (treated same as `[*]` for schema purposes)

**Error handling:** Raise `ValueError` for invalid paths (no `$` prefix, empty property names, unmatched brackets).

```python
"""Utility to convert JSONPath expressions to JSON Schema for constrained decoding."""

import json
import re
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Tuple


def json_path_to_json_schema(json_path: str) -> str:
    """Convert a JSONPath expression to a JSON Schema string.

    The generated schema ensures the LLM output is valid JSON containing
    the structural path described by the JSONPath expression.

    Supported JSONPath subset:
        - ``$``           root (any valid JSON)
        - ``.key``        property access
        - ``[*]``         array wildcard
        - ``[N]``         array index (treated as wildcard for schema)

    Args:
        json_path: A JSONPath expression starting with ``$``.

    Returns:
        A JSON string representing the equivalent JSON Schema.

    Raises:
        ValueError: If the JSONPath expression is invalid.
    """
    if not isinstance(json_path, str) or not json_path.startswith("$"):
        raise ValueError(
            f"JSONPath must be a string starting with '$', got: {json_path!r}"
        )

    rest = json_path[1:]
    segments = _tokenize(rest)
    schema = _build_schema(segments)
    return json.dumps(schema)


def _tokenize(path: str) -> List[Tuple[str, ...]]:
    segments: List[Tuple[str, ...]] = []
    i = 0
    while i < len(path):
        if path[i] == ".":
            i += 1
            match = re.match(r"[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*", path[i:])
            if not match:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"Invalid property name at position {i} in path '${path}'"
                )
            segments.append(("property", match.group()))
            i += match.end()
        elif path[i] == "[":
            close = path.find("]", i)
            if close == -1:
                raise ValueError(f"Unmatched '[' at position {i} in path '${path}'")
            segments.append(("array",))
            i = close + 1
        else:
            raise ValueError(
                f"Unexpected character '{path[i]}' at position {i} in path '${path}'"
            )
    return segments


def _build_schema(segments: List[Tuple[str, ...]]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    if not segments:
        return {}
    seg = segments[0]
    inner = _build_schema(segments[1:])
    if seg[0] == "property":
        return {
            "type": "object",
            "required": [seg[1]],
            "properties": {seg[1]: inner},
        }
    elif seg[0] == "array":
        return {
            "type": "array",
            "items": inner,
        }
    else:
        raise ValueError(f"Unknown segment type: {seg[0]}")
```

]

    )
```

This converts before the existing condition check, so the existing `json_schema` processing path handles everything downstream. No other changes to this method needed.

    rest = json_path[1:]
    segments = _tokenize(rest)
    schema = _build_schema(segments)
    return json.dumps(schema)


def _tokenize(path: str) -> List[Tuple[str, ...]]:
    segments: List[Tuple[str, ...]] = []
    i = 0
    while i < len(path):
        if path[i] == ".":
            i += 1
            match = re.match(r"[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*", path[i:])
            if not match:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"Invalid property name at position {i} in path '${path}'"
                )
            segments.append(("property", match.group()))
            i += match.end()
        elif path[i] == "[":
            close = path.find("]", i)
            if close == -1:
                raise ValueError(f"Unmatched '[' at position {i} in path '${path}'")
            segments.append(("array",))
            i = close + 1
        else:
            raise ValueError(
                f"Unexpected character '{path[i]}' at position {i} in path '${path}'"
            )
    return segments


def _build_schema(segments: List[Tuple[str, ...]]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    if not segments:
        return {}
    seg = segments[0]
    inner = _build_schema(segments[1:])
    if seg[0] == "property":
        return {
            "type": "object",
            "required": [seg[1]],
            "properties": {seg[1]: inner},
        }
    elif seg[0] == "array":
        return {
            "type": "array",
            "items": inner,
        }
    else:
        raise ValueError(f"Unknown segment type: {seg[0]}")
```

Unit tests for the converter:

```python
import json
import unittest
from sglang.srt.constrained.json_path import json_path_to_json_schema

class TestJsonPathToJsonSchema(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_root_only(self):
        self.assertEqual(json.loads(json_path_to_json_schema("$")), {})

    def test_simple_property(self):
        schema = json.loads(json_path_to_json_schema("$.name"))
        self.assertEqual(schema["type"], "object")
        self.assertIn("name", schema["required"])

    def test_nested_properties(self):
        schema = json.loads(json_path_to_json_schema("$.store.book"))
        self.assertEqual(schema["properties"]["store"]["properties"]["book"], {})

    def test_array_with_item_property(self):
        schema = json.loads(json_path_to_json_schema("$.store.book[*].author"))
        book = schema["properties"]["store"]["properties"]["book"]
        self.assertEqual(book["type"], "array")
        self.assertIn("author", book["items"]["required"])

    def test_root_array(self):
        schema = json.loads(json_path_to_json_schema("$[*]"))
        self.assertEqual(schema["type"], "array")

    def test_invalid_no_dollar(self):
        with self.assertRaises(ValueError):
            json_path_to_json_schema("store.book")

    def test_invalid_empty_property(self):
        with self.assertRaises(ValueError):
            json_path_to_json_schema("$.")

    def test_invalid_unmatched_bracket(self):
        with self.assertRaises(ValueError):
            json_path_to_json_schema("$.store[")
```

4. **API surface:** `python -c "from sglang.lang.api import gen; g = gen('x', json_path='$.name'); print(g.sampling_params.json_path)"` — should print `$.name`

18	class SglSamplingParams:
19	    max_new_tokens: int = 128
20	    min_new_tokens: int = 0
21	    n: int = 1
22	    stop: Union[str, List[str]] = ()
23	    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = ()
24	    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = ()
25	    temperature: float = 1.0
26	    top_p: float = 1.0
27	    top_k: int = -1  # -1 means disable
28	    min_p: float = 0.0
29	    frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
30	    presence_penalty: float = 0.0
31	    ignore_eos: bool = False
32	    return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None
33	    logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = (None,)
34	    top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = (None,)
35	    return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = (None,)
36	    json_schema: Optional[str] = None
37	
38	    # for constrained generation, not included in to_xxx_kwargs
39	    dtype: Optional[str] = None
40	    regex: Optional[str] = None

42	    def clone(self):
43	        return SglSamplingParams(
44	            self.max_new_tokens,
45	            self.min_new_tokens,
46	            self.n,
47	            self.stop,
48	            self.stop_token_ids,
49	            self.stop_regex,
50	            self.temperature,
51	            self.top_p,
52	            self.top_k,
53	            self.min_p,
54	            self.frequency_penalty,
55	            self.presence_penalty,
56	            self.ignore_eos,

64	    def to_openai_kwargs(self):
65	        # OpenAI does not support top_k, so we drop it here
66	        if self.regex is not None:
67	            warnings.warn("Regular expression is not supported in the OpenAI backend.")
68	        return {
69	            "max_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
70	            "max_completion_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
71	            "n": self.n,
72	            "stop": self.stop or None,
73	            "temperature": self.temperature,
74	            "top_p": self.top_p,
75	            "frequency_penalty": self.frequency_penalty,
76	            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,
77	        }

79	    def to_vertexai_kwargs(self):
80	        if self.regex is not None:
81	            warnings.warn(
82	                "Regular expression is not supported in the VertexAI backend."
83	            )
84	        return {
85	            "candidate_count": 1,
86	            "max_output_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
87	            "stop_sequences": self.stop,
88	            "temperature": self.temperature,
89	            "top_p": self.top_p,
90	            "top_k": self.top_k if self.top_k > 0 else None,
91	        }

93	    def to_anthropic_kwargs(self):
94	        # Anthropic does not support frequency_penalty or presence_penalty, so we drop it here
95	        if self.regex is not None:
96	            warnings.warn(
97	                "Regular expression is not supported in the Anthropic backend."
98	            )
99	        return {
100	            "max_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
101	            "stop_sequences": (
102	                self.stop if isinstance(self.stop, (list, tuple)) else [self.stop]

105	            "top_p": self.top_p,
106	            "top_k": self.top_k,
107	        }

141	class SglFunction:
142	    def __init__(self, func, num_api_spec_tokens=None, bind_arguments=None):
143	        self.func = func
144	        self.num_api_spec_tokens = num_api_spec_tokens
145	        self.bind_arguments = bind_arguments or {}
146	        self.pin_prefix_rid = None
147	
148	        # Parse arguments
149	        argspec = inspect.getfullargspec(func)
150	        assert argspec.args[0] == "s", 'The first argument must be "s"'
151	        self.arg_names = argspec.args[1:]
152	        self.arg_defaults = argspec.defaults if argspec.defaults is not None else []
153	
154	    def bind(self, **kwargs):
155	        assert all(key in self.arg_names for key in kwargs)
156	
157	        new_bind_dict = {**self.bind_arguments, **kwargs}
158	        return SglFunction(self.func, bind_arguments=new_bind_dict)
159	
160	    def run(
161	        self,
162	        *args,
163	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
164	        n: int = 1,
165	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
166	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
167	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
168	        temperature: float = 1.0,
169	        top_p: float = 1.0,
170	        top_k: int = -1,
171	        min_p: float = 0.0,
172	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
173	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
174	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
175	        return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
176	        logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
177	        top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
178	        return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
179	        stream: bool = False,
180	        backend=None,
181	        use_thread: bool = True,
182	        **kwargs,
183	    ):
184	        from sglang.lang.interpreter import run_program
185	
186	        # avoid using [] as the default arg: https://nikos7am.com/posts/mutable-default-arguments/
187	        if stop is None:
188	            stop = []
189	        if stop_token_ids is None:
190	            stop_token_ids = []
191	        if stop_regex is None:
192	            stop_regex = []
193	
194	        default_sampling_para = SglSamplingParams(
195	            max_new_tokens=max_new_tokens,
196	            n=n,
197	            stop=stop,
198	            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
199	            stop_regex=stop_regex,
200	            temperature=temperature,
201	            top_p=top_p,
202	            top_k=top_k,
203	            min_p=min_p,
204	            frequency_penalty=frequency_penalty,
205	            presence_penalty=presence_penalty,
206	            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
207	            return_logprob=return_logprob,
208	            logprob_start_len=logprob_start_len,
209	            top_logprobs_num=top_logprobs_num,
210	            return_text_in_logprobs=return_text_in_logprobs,
211	        )
212	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
213	        return run_program(
214	            self,
215	            backend,
216	            args,
217	            kwargs,
218	            default_sampling_para,
219	            stream,
220	            use_thread=use_thread,
221	        )
222	
223	    def run_batch(
224	        self,
225	        batch_kwargs,
226	        *,
227	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
228	        n: int = 1,
229	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
230	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
231	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
232	        temperature: float = 1.0,
233	        top_p: float = 1.0,
234	        top_k: int = -1,
235	        min_p: float = 0.0,
236	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
237	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
238	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
239	        return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
240	        logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
241	        top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
242	        return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
243	        backend=None,
244	        num_threads: Union[str, int] = "auto",
245	        progress_bar: bool = False,
246	        generator_style: bool = False,
247	    ):
248	        from sglang.lang.interpreter import run_program_batch
249	
250	        if stop is None:
251	            stop = []
252	        if stop_token_ids is None:
253	            stop_token_ids = []
254	        if stop_regex is None:
255	            stop_regex = []
256	
257	        assert isinstance(batch_kwargs, (list, tuple))
258	        if len(batch_kwargs) == 0:
259	            return []
260	        if not isinstance(batch_kwargs[0], dict):
261	            num_programs = len(batch_kwargs)
262	            # change the list of argument values to dict of arg_name -> arg_value
263	            batch_kwargs = [
264	                {self.arg_names[i]: v for i, v in enumerate(arg_values)}
265	                for arg_values in batch_kwargs
266	                if isinstance(arg_values, (list, tuple))
267	                and len(self.arg_names) - len(self.arg_defaults)
268	                <= len(arg_values)
269	                <= len(self.arg_names)
270	            ]
271	            # Ensure to raise an exception if the number of arguments mismatch
272	            if len(batch_kwargs) != num_programs:
273	                raise Exception("Given arguments mismatch the SGL function signature")
274	
275	        default_sampling_para = SglSamplingParams(
276	            max_new_tokens=max_new_tokens,
277	            n=n,
278	            stop=stop,
279	            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
280	            stop_regex=stop_regex,
281	            temperature=temperature,
282	            top_p=top_p,
283	            top_k=top_k,
284	            min_p=min_p,
285	            frequency_penalty=frequency_penalty,
286	            presence_penalty=presence_penalty,
287	            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
288	            return_logprob=return_logprob,
289	            logprob_start_len=logprob_start_len,
290	            top_logprobs_num=top_logprobs_num,
291	            return_text_in_logprobs=return_text_in_logprobs,
292	        )
293	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
294	        return run_program_batch(
295	            self,
296	            backend,
297	            batch_kwargs,
298	            default_sampling_para,
299	            num_threads,
300	            progress_bar,
301	            generator_style=generator_style,
302	        )
303	
304	    def trace(self, *, backend=None, **kwargs):
305	        from sglang.lang.tracer import trace_program
306	
307	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
308	        return trace_program(self, kwargs, backend)
309	
310	    def cache(self, backend=None):
311	        from sglang.lang.interpreter import cache_program
312	
313	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
314	        return cache_program(self, backend)
315	
316	    def __call__(self, *args, **kwargs):
317	        from sglang.lang.tracer import TracingScope
318	
319	        tracing_scope = TracingScope.get_current_scope()
320	        if tracing_scope is None:
321	            return self.run(*args, **kwargs)
322	        else:
323	            kwargs["backend"] = tracing_scope.tracer_state.backend
324	            return self.trace(*args, **kwargs)

327	class SglExpr:
328	    node_ct = 0
329	
330	    def __init__(self):
331	        self.node_id = SglExpr.node_ct
332	        self.prev_node = None
333	        self.pid = None
334	        SglExpr.node_ct += 1
335	
336	    def __add__(self, other):
337	        if isinstance(other, str):
338	            other = SglConstantText(other)
339	        assert isinstance(other, SglExpr)
340	
341	        return self.concatenate_ir(self, other)
342	
343	    def __radd__(self, other):
344	        if isinstance(other, str):
345	            other = SglConstantText(other)
346	        assert isinstance(other, SglExpr), f"{other}"
347	
348	        return self.concatenate_ir(other, self)
349	
350	    def concatenate_ir(self, a, b):
351	        if isinstance(a, SglExprList):
352	            if isinstance(b, SglExprList):
353	                return SglExprList(a.expr_list + b.expr_list)
354	            else:
355	                return SglExprList(a.expr_list + [b])
356	        elif isinstance(b, SglExprList):
357	            return SglExprList([a] + b.expr_list)
358	
359	        return SglExprList([a, b])
360	
361	    def print_graph_dfs(self):
362	        ret = [""]
363	        visited = set()
364	
365	        def dfs_print(x):
366	            if x is None or x in visited:
367	                return
368	            visited.add(x)
369	
370	            # Print dependency
371	            if x.prev_node is not None:
372	                dfs_print(x.prev_node)
373	
374	            if isinstance(x, SglExprList):
375	                for y in x.expr_list:
376	                    dfs_print(y)
377	            # elif isinstance(x, SglRole):
378	            #    dfs_print(x.expr)
379	            elif isinstance(x, SglVariable):
380	                dfs_print(x.source)
381	
382	            # Print the node itself
383	            if isinstance(x, (SglFork, SglGetForkItem)):
384	                ret[0] += f"%{x.node_id} = {x}\n"
385	            else:
386	                if x.prev_node is not None:
387	                    ret[0] += (
388	                        f"%{x.node_id} = %{x.prev_node.node_id} + " + str(x) + "\n"
389	                    )
390	                else:
391	                    ret[0] += f"%{x.node_id} = " + str(x) + "\n"
392	
393	        dfs_print(self)
394	        return ret[0]
395

619	class SglSeparateReasoning(SglExpr):
620	    def __init__(self, model_type: str, expr: SglExpr):
621	        super().__init__()
622	        self.model_type = model_type
623	
624	        self.expr = expr
625	        self.name = None
626	        self._process_expr(expr)
627	
628	    def process_name_for_reasoning(self, name):
629	        if not name:
630	            raise ValueError("name must be provided")
631	        return f"{name}_reasoning_content"
632	
633	    def _process_expr(self, expr):
634	        if isinstance(expr, SglGen):
635	            self.name = self.process_name_for_reasoning(expr.name)
636	        elif isinstance(expr, SglSelect):
637	            self.name = self.process_name_for_reasoning(expr.name)
638	        elif isinstance(expr, SglExprList):
639	            for x in expr.expr_list:
640	                self._process_expr(x)
641	
642	    def __repr__(self):
643	        return f"SeparateReasoning(model_type={self.model_type}, name={self.name})"

76	    name: Optional[str] = None,
77	    max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
78	    min_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
79	    n: Optional[int] = None,
80	    stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
81	    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
82	    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
83	    temperature: Optional[float] = None,
84	    top_p: Optional[float] = None,
85	    top_k: Optional[int] = None,
86	    min_p: Optional[float] = None,
87	    frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
88	    presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
89	    ignore_eos: Optional[bool] = None,
90	    return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
91	    logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
92	    top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
93	    return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,

96	    choices_method: Optional[ChoicesSamplingMethod] = None,
97	    regex: Optional[str] = None,
98	    json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
99	):
100	    """Call the model to generate. See the meaning of the arguments in docs/backend/sampling_params.md"""
101	
102	    if choices:
103	        return SglSelect(
104	            name,
105	            choices,
106	            0.0 if temperature is None else temperature,
107	            token_length_normalized if choices_method is None else choices_method,
108	        )
109	
110	    # check regex is valid

117	    return SglGen(

121	        n,

134	        top_logprobs_num,
135	        return_text_in_logprobs,
136	        dtype,
137	        regex,

139	    )
